April 9, 1908.

REPORT OF COMMITTEE ON THE CURRICULUM (Continued)

I. Thet the following named persons be given degrees &S of the

Class of 1907:

Leslie ftordon Lithgow Clapham Murray, Jr.

)

; the degree of Bachelor of Aris. Williem Lloyd Keepers ; )

Douglas Halsted

Laurence Roberts Carton )

)the degree of Bachelor of Letters. Delos Crary )

Frederic Beekman McCracken - the degree of Civil Engineer

ks Thet Charles Dallas Ridgway, Jr., be given the degree of Civil Engineer, as of the Cless of 1905.

III. That Ralph Alden Garrison (A. B. Prineeton, 1905) be

given the degree of Master of Arts on Examinsation.

IV. Thet Ewing Lawrence Rafferty, of the Class of 1906, whose diploma was withheld, because on the day of his class' graduation, he ap- peared on the Commencement platform when the names of cendidates for the Bachelor's degrees were called, although at the time he had not qualified

for his degree, be now granted his diploma.

V. That the substitution, in the second term of the present

academic year, of & Senior Course on Money and Banking, and Trusts, by 7

Professor Meeker, for the course by Professor Wyckoff, known as Economies

50 whieh had to be withdrewn because of the sudden and serious impairment

of Professor Wyckoff's health, be approved.

B. On recommendation by the President of the University

L. That Guy Chester Crampton, A. B. Princeton, 1904, M. A ? P T

Cornell, 1905, be appointed Assistant to Professor Conklin, in the Depart-

ment of Biology, on a salary

of $800, the Finance Committee approving.
